availableModes.name: build
availableModes.description: The default agent. Executes tools based on configured permissions.

availableModes.name: plan
availableModes.description: Plan mode. Disallows all edit tools.

availableCommands.name: frontend-design
availableCommands.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

availableCommands.name: go-code-quality
availableCommands.description: Required to learn how to read and write Go code the way the user wants

availableCommands.name: android-code-quality
availableCommands.description: Required to learn how to read and write Android and Kotlin code the way the user wants

availableCommands.name: typescript-code-quality
availableCommands.description: Required to learn how to read and write TypeScript code the way the user wants

availableCommands.name: browse
availableCommands.description:
Browse and interact with web pages using Chrome DevTools MCP. Use this skill when:
(1) Navigating to URLs to inspect web pages
(2) Taking screenshots of web pages
(3) Clicking elements, filling forms, or interacting with UI
(4) Inspecting DOM, console logs, or network requests
(5) Debugging frontend issues or testing web applications
(6) Verifying UI rendering or behavior
Requires Chrome running with --remote-debugging-port=9222

availableCommands.name: skill-creator
availableCommands.description: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends the coding agent's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.

availableCommands.name: skill-installer
availableCommands.description: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos).

availableCommands.name: ask-questions-if-underspecified
availableCommands.description: Clarify requirements before implementing. Use when serious doubts araise.

availableCommands.name: code-quality
availableCommands.description: Required to learn how to read and write ANY code the way the user wants

content.text: The user is asking me to provide detailed information about Canada. This

is a straightforward request for factual information about a country. Let me provide a comprehensive response about Canada.

I don't need to use any tools for this - it's general knowledge that I can share. Let me provide a detailed overview of Canada.

Canada is a country in North America, known for its vast landscapes, multicultural population, and high quality of life. Here's a comprehensive overview:

## Geography and Land

Canada is the **second-largest country in the world by land area**, covering approximately 9.98 million square kilometers. It stretches from the Atlantic Ocean in the east to the Pacific Ocean in the west, and from the United States border in the south to the Arctic Ocean in the north. Canada has the **longest coastline** of any nation in the world (over 202,000 km). The country includes diverse terrain: the Rocky Mountains, vast boreal forests, prairies, tundra, lakes, and rivers. Notable natural landmarks include Niagara Falls, the Great Lakes, Banff National Park, and the Canadian Shield.

## Population and Demographics

Canada has a population of approximately **38 million people**. It is one of the most sparsely populated nations, with the

majority living in urban areas concentrated near the U.S. border. Major cities include Toronto, Montreal, Vancouver, Calgary, and Ottawa (the capital). Canada is renowned for its multiculturalism, with immigrants from around

the world. The population is diverse: English Canadians, French Canadians (particularly in Quebec), Indigenous peoples (First Nations, Inuit, Métis), and numerous ethnic communities.

## Government and Politics

Canada is a **constitutional monarchy** with a parliamentary democracy. The head of state is the British monarch (currently King Charles III), represented by the Governor General. The Prime Minister is the head of government. The country operates as a federal parliamentary republic with 10 provinces and 3 territories. Federal elections use a Westminster-style parliamentary system. Major political parties include the Liberal Party, Conservative Party, NDP, and Bloc Québécois.

## History

Indigenous peoples have inhabited Canada for thousands of years. European exploration began in the late 15th century. Canada was founded through the British colonization of New France and various British colonies. The **Constitution Act of 1867** established Canada as

a dominion within the British Empire. Canada gained full independence through the Statute of Westminster (1931) and the Constitution Act (1982). Key historical events include the Loyalist migration, the fur trade, the creation of the Canadian Pacific Railway, and the Quiet Revolution in Quebec.

## Economy

Canada has one of the **world's largest economies**, with a GDP of approximately $2 trillion. It is a member of the G7 and OECD. Key industries include:
- **Natural resources**: Oil, natural gas, minerals, lumber
-

**Manufacturing**: Automotive, aerospace, machinery
- **Services**: Finance, technology, tourism
- **Agriculture**: Wheat, canola, dairy, livestock

Canada is a major oil producer and has significant hydroelectric capacity. The Canadian dollar (CAD) is the seventh-most traded currency globally. Major trading partners include the United States (by far the largest), China, and the UK.

##

Culture and Society

Canada is famous for its **politeness, diversity, and inclusivity**. Official languages are **English and French** (bilingualism is constitutionally protected). The Canadian identity is shaped by:
- **Hockey**: The national winter sport
- **Maple syrup**: A iconic Canadian product
- **Multiculturalism policy**: Officially promoted since 1971
- **Universal healthcare**: Publicly funded medical care
- **Social welfare**: Strong social safety nets

Canadian culture blends British, French, and Indigenous influences, particularly in Quebec

where French-Canadian culture thrives. Canada also has vibrant Indigenous cultures with significant contributions to art, music, and spirituality.

## Education and Social Services

Canada has a highly educated population with free or subsidized education at various levels. Primary and secondary education is free in public schools. Post-secondary education is heavily subsidized, though students pay tuition. Canada has world-renowned universities (University of Toronto, McGill, University of British Columbia). The country maintains universal healthcare and strong social

programs including unemployment insurance and pension plans.

## International Relations

Canada is an active member of the United Nations, NATO, the Commonwealth, La Francophonie, and the G20. It participates in peacekeeping missions globally. Canada is known for its foreign aid and humanitarian contributions. It maintains a close relationship with the United States through trade agreements (USMCA) and defense ties.

## Climate and Environment

Canada experiences diverse climates from temperate in the south to arctic in the north. Winters are cold and long in most regions;

summers are warm. Environmental issues include climate change, deforestation, and resource management. Canada is committed to emissions reduction and protecting natural habitats, with extensive national parks and conservation efforts.

## Symbolism

National symbols include the **maple leaf** (on the flag), the beaver, the loon (common loon), and "O Canada" (the national anthem). The flag features a red and white color scheme with a stylized 11-point maple leaf in the center.

Canada consistently ranks highly in quality of life, education, economic competitiveness, and social development indices. It remains a prominent global actor known for its progressive policies and natural beauty.
